commits.message: Revamp README with clearer structure and formatting
commits.full_message:
Revamp README with clearer structure and formatting

- Add badges for Python version and license
- Organize into clear sections: capabilities, setup, options
- Add permissions table for Discord bot setup
- Add CLI options reference table
- Credit original author

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: David Drake

commits.message: Ensure export file is valid before starting
commits.full_message:
Ensure export file is valid before starting

Produces a much better error than a generic `FileNotFoundError` when the
import initially attempts to access `users.json`.

Also transparently handles the case where the export file has been
modified and the export contents have accidentally been nested under a
top-level folder.

Fixes #37
commits.author: Carey Metcalfe

commits.message: Prevent failed cached streams from raising exceptions when being closed
commits.full_message:
Prevent failed cached streams from raising exceptions when being closed

If the initial request fails, the `self._cache` attribute of the
`CachedSeekableHTTPStream` would not be set. This would cause the
`close()` method to raise an `AttributeError` when it was called as part
of the `io.BufferedIOBase`'s `__del__` method. Fortunately, because the
exception was raised in the destructor, it was ignored, but still logged
a warning.

This commit fixes the issue by ensuring the `_cache` attribute is set on
the object before making the initial request and potentially raising an
exception.
commits.author: Carey Metcalfe
